headline: Core Ethereum Lido contracts are explorer-verified, source-linked, and covered by recent recognized audits.

findings.text: The assessed Ethereum mainnet Lido/stETH proxy at 0xae7ab96520DE3A18E5e111B5EaAb095312D7fE84 is verified as an AppProxyUpgradeable similar match and points to implementation 0x6ca84080381E43938476814be61B779A8bB6a600; the implementation is also source-code verified. The assessed WithdrawalQueueERC721 proxy at 0x889edC2eDab5f40e902b864aD4d7AdE8E412F9B1 is verified as an OssifiableProxy similar match and points to implementation 0xE42C659Dc09109566720EA8b2De186c2Be7D94D9; the implementation is source-code verified as an exact match.

Lido docs link the assessed Lido and WithdrawalQueueERC721 contracts to the public lidofinance/core repository at release v3.0.2; the v3.0.2 GitHub release identifies commit 2a2210b, and the repository contains the corresponding Lido.sol and WithdrawalQueueERC721.sol files under the expected compiler-version directories.

The public audit index and v3.0.2 release identify recent Lido V3 audits: Certora and MixBytes reports dated 03-2026 are linked from the v3.0.2 release, while the audit index also lists 12-2025 Certora, MixBytes, and Consensys Diligence Lido V3 reports with issue summaries.
The auditors supporting the current Lido V3 review set include Certora, MixBytes, and Consensys Diligence, all recognized Solidity/security-review firms under this rubric.

The deployed-contracts page reports Ethereum mainnet production Lido protocol version v3.0.2, and the v3.0.2 release links March 2026 Certora and MixBytes audit artifacts, so the visible audit coverage is within roughly one month of the assessed production version.

For both proxied contracts assessed, Etherscan exposes the proxy and its implementation: the stETH proxy points to a verified Lido implementation, and the WithdrawalQueue proxy points to a verified exact-match WithdrawalQueueERC721 implementation.

steelman.red: A red grade would be strongest if the current implementations behind Lido's proxies were unverified or if no public repository or audit artifacts existed, but the assessed proxy and implementation contracts are verified and public audit artifacts exist.
steelman.orange: An orange grade would be strongest because I did not run an independent bytecode diff against the GitHub release and did not exhaustively inspect every satellite contract in the full Lido V3 deployment.
steelman.green: A green grade is strongest because the assessed core Ethereum Lido/stETH and WithdrawalQueue proxy-plus-implementation contracts are explorer-verified, linked to public source in the lidofinance/core v3.0.2 release, and supported by recent Certora and MixBytes Lido V3 audit artifacts.

rationale.verdict: Choosing green because the main assessed contracts have verified proxy and verified implementation source on Etherscan, the public docs map them to lidofinance/core v3.0.2 source files, and the v3.0.2 release links recent recognized-firm audit artifacts; the remaining gaps are scope limits around independent bytecode diffing and exhaustive satellite-contract coverage, not evidence of unverified code.

evidence.shows: Ethereum mainnet production deployment page listing Lido protocol version v3.0.2 and core contract addresses including Lido/stETH proxy, implementation, WithdrawalQueueERC721 proxy, and other production contracts.
Lido/stETH token proxy source code is verified as a similar match for AppProxyUpgradeable and exposes implementation 0x6ca84080381E43938476814be61B779A8bB6a600.
Current Lido/stETH implementation contract is source-code verified on Etherscan as contract name Lido.
WithdrawalQueueERC721 proxy source code is verified as a similar match for OssifiableProxy and exposes implementation 0xE42C659Dc09109566720EA8b2De186c2Be7D94D9.
Current WithdrawalQueueERC721 implementation contract is source-code verified on Etherscan as an exact match.
Lido core v3.0.2 release identifies commit 2a2210b and links 03-2026 Certora and MixBytes Lido V3 audit artifacts.
Public source repository contains Lido.sol in the v3.0.2 release under contracts/0.4.24, corresponding to the explorer-visible Lido implementation contract name and compiler family.

Public source repository contains WithdrawalQueueERC721.sol in the v3.0.2 release under contracts/0.8.9, corresponding to the explorer-visible WithdrawalQueueERC721 implementation contract name and compiler family.

Lido audit index lists recognized-firm Lido V3 reviews, including 12-2025 Certora Lido V3 Security Audit, 12-2025 Certora Lido V3 Formal Verification, 12-2025 MixBytes Lido V3 Security Audit, and 12-2025 Consensys Diligence Lido V3 Security Audit, plus older Lido V2 audits and deployment validation notes.
Certora Lido V3 smart-contract security assessment fix-review artifact linked from the v3.0.2 release.
MixBytes Lido V3 security audit report artifact linked from the v3.0.2 release.

Lido docs describe the Lido contract as the liquid staking pool and stETH rebasing token, accepting ETH stake, minting stETH, handling accounting, fees, rewards, withdrawals, and links to source and deployed contract.
WithdrawalQueueERC721 docs describe the FIFO stETH withdrawal queue, unstETH NFT, request, finalization, and claim flow, and link to source and deployed contract.
Lido DAO docs describe DAO governance, LDO voting token governance, governance forum, and LDO token contract address.
Immunefi page verifies Lido's public bug bounty program, maximum bounty, live status, last update date, and smart-contract/application scope.
Lido Safe Harbor documentation gives safeharbor@lido.fi as a designated security contact for live active-exploit recovery coordination.

unknowns: V2: I did not run an independent local compile or bytecode-diff between Etherscan bytecode and the lidofinance/core v3.0.2 repository.
V3: I opened the 03-2026 Certora and MixBytes audit artifact pages but did not parse the PDFs deeply enough to enumerate every contract and commit in scope.
V5: I did not exhaustively diff all code changes between the March 2026 audit artifacts and the v3.0.2 release; I relied on the v3.0.2 release linking those audits as release audit evidence.
V1: I assessed the core Lido/stETH and WithdrawalQueueERC721 contracts plus their implementations, not every satellite contract listed on the production deployment page.

protocol_metadata.about: Lido lets users stake ETH into a pooled validator set and receive stETH, a rebasing liquid staking receipt token that can be used across DeFi while staking rewards accrue. The core Lido contract accepts ETH, mints stETH, processes oracle-driven accounting and rewards, and routes buffered ETH toward withdrawals or staking. Exiting through the protocol uses WithdrawalQueueERC721: users lock stETH or wstETH into a FIFO queue, receive an unstETH NFT claim ticket, and claim ETH after oracle finalization.
